mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative or flat 60-day forward returns in over 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, a sudden positive deviation in the index signals supply-side stabilization that reverses bearish equity positioning and generates positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO turns positive after 3 consecutive months of decline
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns fail to materialize in more than 50% of confirmed supply stabilization events, or the signal performs identically during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity that supports corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS against 5-day forward returns remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows, or positive payroll surprises consistently coincide with negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPI exceeds 3.0 percent, anticipated monetary tightening compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL breaches the 225.0 threshold, or inflation spikes fail to predict equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates weak consumer demand despite headline growth, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment, negative industrial production growth confirms economic weakness that triggers immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 40% of instances where the trigger condition is met, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment falls below 7.0%.
